question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of write-isolation enforcement in the read-only TransactionTemplate to observe partially-committed tampered transaction data mid-flight, causing stale or inconsistent topic message state to be streamed to subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of concurrent subscribeTopic gRPC calls that each open a read-only transaction, exhausting the database connection pool and preventing legitimate transaction reads from completing, effectively denying access to submitted transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the @Qualifier('readOnly') TransactionTemplate does not enforce row-level locking to race against a transaction write and read tampered intermediate transaction state before it is committed, causing incorrect topic message data to be returned?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a subscribeTopic request that triggers repeated read-only transaction retries by supplying a startTime near the current time boundary, causing the TransactionTemplate to repeatedly re-execute queries and amplify database load, impacting the integrity of transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the read-only TransactionTemplate bean to observe uncommitted transaction data if the underlying database isolation level is READ_UNCOMMITTED, allowing them to see tampered in-flight submitted transactions before consensus?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user open exactly maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection (default 5) long-lived streaming subscriptions per TCP connection to saturate the per-connection call slot, preventing other users from submitting or observing transactions on that connection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user open many TCP connections each consuming maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection slots with infinite-limit subscriptions, exhausting the applicationTaskExecutor thread pool and stalling transaction processing pipelines?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared applicationTaskExecutor passed to serverBuilder.executor() to starve legitimate transaction-processing tasks by flooding the executor with slow-consuming gRPC streaming calls, causing submitted transactions to be delayed or dropped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a subscribeTopic request with no endTime and no limit, holding a streaming connection open indefinitely and consuming one of the maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection slots, preventing other callers from receiving submitted transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-IP or per-user connection rate limiting in the NettyServerBuilder configuration to open an unbounded number of TCP connections, each with maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection active streams, amplifying resource exhaustion against the transaction read path?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of TLS enforcement in the NettyServerBuilder customizer to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the gRPC channel, intercepting and tampering with submitted transaction responses in transit?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a malformed gRPC frame that bypasses the maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection check by exploiting HTTP/2 stream multiplexing, causing the server to process more concurrent calls than intended and corrupting transaction state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a SETTINGS frame with a very large INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE to the Netty server to force the server to buffer large amounts of transaction response data in memory, causing an out-of-memory condition that corrupts in-flight transaction delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maxInboundMessageSize limit in the NettyServerBuilder customizer to send an oversized ConsensusTopicQuery protobuf message, causing heap exhaustion that disrupts transaction processing for all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maxInboundMetadataSize limit in the NettyServerBuilder customizer to send oversized gRPC headers/metadata, causing memory pressure that impacts the transaction read pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a keepAliveTimeout configuration in the NettyServerBuilder to hold zombie connections open indefinitely, consuming maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection slots and blocking legitimate transaction subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maxConnectionAge limit in the NettyServerBuilder customizer to maintain a single long-lived connection that monopolizes executor threads, preventing timely delivery of submitted transaction data to other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maxConnectionIdle limit in the NettyServerBuilder customizer to keep idle connections alive indefinitely, accumulating resource usage that degrades transaction throughput for legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send rapid HTTP/2 RST_STREAM frames to reset streams immediately after opening them, cycling through maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection slots faster than the server can clean them up, causing a resource leak that impacts transaction delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared applicationTaskExecutor to submit tasks that block indefinitely by subscribing to a topic with no endTime and a very old startTime, causing the executor to queue up historical transaction retrieval tasks that never complete?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the read-only TransactionTemplate to perform a long-running read transaction that holds a database snapshot open, preventing VACUUM or garbage collection on the transaction table and causing storage bloat that degrades write performance for submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated read-only transaction executions by subscribing to a topic with a very old startTime, causing the TransactionTemplate to execute many sequential DB queries that hold read locks and interfere with concurrent transaction writes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection is applied per-connection (not globally) to open thousands of connections each with 5 active streams, bypassing the intended concurrency limit and overwhelming the transaction processing pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of flow control configuration in the NettyServerBuilder to flood the server with back-pressure-ignoring subscriptions, causing the reactive pipeline to buffer unbounded transaction messages in memory and crash the JVM?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-client rate limiting in the NettyServerBuilder configuration to send thousands of subscribeTopic requests per second, causing the transaction read path to be overwhelmed and legitimate transaction data to be delayed?
